channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Judge says he will temporarily block Trump from placing thousands of USAID workers on paid leave
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday dealt President Donald Trump and billionaire ally Elon Musk their first big setback in their dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development, saying he will order a temporary halt to plans to pull thousands of agency staffers off the job. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chairman
item.description: President Donald Trump says he is firing members of the board of trustees for the Kennedy Center and naming himself chairman.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he's ending Biden's classified intelligence briefings in payback move
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Friday that he's revoking former President Joe Biden's access to government secrets and ending the daily intelligence briefings he's receiving in payback for Biden doing the same to him in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Latino group calls for boycott of Coca-Cola, Walmart and more over deportations
item.description: This is part of an effort by a group called Freeze Latino Movement to boycott American companies while poking fun of the ongoing deportations launched by the Trump Administration and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KTLA 5

item.title: Intoxicated Virginia man allegedly breaks into Bible study session thinking it was human trafficking operation
item.description: A Virginia man was arrested after breaking into a Bible study session he thought was a human trafficking operations, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: West Virginia leads 22 states suing over New York Climate Change Superfund Act
item.description: West Virginia led the charge against New York’s Climate Change Superfund Act, suing to block the law.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he'll sign order promoting plastic straws
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he plans to sign an executive order promoting plastic straws after his predecessor sought to phase out single-use plastics from government operations.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP-led House panel to hold hearing on USAID
item.description: The GOP-led House Foreign Affairs Committee will hold a hearing on the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) on Feb. 13, as the Trump administration moved to effectively shut down the agency and institute a mass foreign aid freeze. Committee Chair Brian Mast (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Reid G. Miller, longtime AP international correspondent and editor, dies at 90
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Reid G. Miller, who traveled the planet as an intrepid international correspondent for The Associated Press and developed a reputation as a supportive editor and unswervingly loyal boss during the toughest of breaking-news moments, has died. He was 90.
item.dc:creator: TED ANTHONY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOJ appeals block of birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: It's the first Justice Department appeal of a lawsuit challenging major Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Project 2025 architect Russell Vought to lead powerful White House budget office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Russell Vought as White House budget director on Thursday night, putting an official who has planned the zealous expansion of President Donald Trump's power into one of the most influential positions in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Joe Rogan cheers Musk’s ‘business approach’ to government cuts
item.description: “What’s fascinating right now is we’re getting a chance to see what happens when you take a business approach to the government,” Rogan said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: 5 questions about DOGE's access to federal payment systems
item.description: Questions are swirling about how much access Elon Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) has to Americans’ personal data and sensitive federal payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Real estate agent commissions mostly unchanged since new NAR rules
item.description: New real estate commission rules took effect in August, but so far, not much has changed, according to a Redfin report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Arrest made in home invasion where men dressed as Amazon workers
item.description: The Savannah Police Department (SPD) has made an arrest in the home invasion that sent one man to the hospital in early January.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NCAA changes transgender policy to limit women's competition to athletes assigned female at birth
item.description: The NCAA changed its participation policy for transgender athletes on Thursday, limiting competition in women's sports to athletes assigned female at birth only.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Austin ISD policy prevents parents from seeing video of alleged crimes against kids
item.description: He said his first-grade teacher trapped him behind a classroom door at Margaret T. Reilly Elementary School and then pushed on it with his weight.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Wiley, NewsNation

item.title: Public objections stall State Library defunding bill
item.description: “I did receive a lot of letters and the outpouring from the hearts of the little children were really, really special to me,” Rep. Lana Greenfield said.
item.dc:creator: Gracie Terrall, NewsNation

item.title: Former DHS employee arrested for kidnapping child, falsifying documents
item.description: Xander Faison showed an emergency custody form and said if the child was not handed over, police would take her away.
item.dc:creator: Sydnee Batzlaff/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Southern California community is sliding toward the ocean, NASA data shows
item.description: The residential area shifted toward the Pacific Ocean as much as 4 inches — per week — during a four-week period last fall.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: Superintendent forum interrupted by pornographic image
item.description: An online superintendent forum at noon Tuesday was interrupted by a pornographic image, according to a spokesperson with the Liberty County School System.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Applebee’s brings back Date Night Passes
item.description: (KTLA) - Applebee’s announced Thursday that it's bringing back the coveted Date Night Pass, which went viral on social media during its debut last year. This year, the chain is offering 3,000 passes that will be exclusively available to Club Applebee’s members.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Justice Sotomayor renews her opposition to the court's ruling that ex-presidents have broad immunity
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — U.S. Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor said her conservative colleagues are risking the court's legitmacy with decisions affording President Donald Trump broad immunity and overturning longstanding precedents on other issues.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump doubles down on Gaza Strip idea, says no US soldiers would be needed
item.description: The president in an early Thursday post expanded on his idea, making clear he was serious after some advisers sought to clarify or temper aspects of the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: 2nd victim dies after shooting at Ohio cosmetics facility; suspect appears in court
item.description: A second person has died after a gunman opened fire in a cosmetics facility in New Albany, Ohio, late on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, Fox 5

item.title: Southern California community is sliding toward the ocean, NASA data shows
item.description: The residential area shifted toward the Pacific Ocean as much as 4 inches — per week — during a four-week period last fall.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Protesters in cities across the US rally against Trump's policies, Project 2025 and Elon Musk
item.description: Demonstrators gathered in cities across the U.S. on Wednesday to protest the Trump administration's early actions, decrying everything from the president's immigration crackdown to his rollback of transgender rights and a proposal to forcibly transfer Palestinians from the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CIA shares new employee names through unclassified email
item.description: The CIA used unclassified email to share a list of employees hired within the last two years to the Office of Personnel Management (OPM).
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: New NASA imagery shows a large asteroid could impact Earth in 2032
item.description: A recently released animation shows a new look at a large asteroid that has a small, but significant, chance of impacting planet Earth in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KTLA 5

item.title: Man jailed over police AI program, then freed 17 months after victim raised doubts
item.description: Chris Gatlin spent 17 months in jail for a crime that an artificial intelligence program said he committed, only to be freed after the prosecutor learned there was no real evidence.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, Fox 5

item.title: White House defends Trump's Gaza takeover, resettlement proposal
item.description: The White House on Wednesday defended President Trump’s proposal for the United States to take control of the Gaza Strip amid blowback from Arab leaders and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In bid for Davos-like gathering, Semafor plans to bring at least 200 CEOs to Washington conference
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The media company Semafor is planning a Washington economic conference for global CEOs this April that it hopes will rival the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, for influence.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Postal Service flip-flops on Hong Kong-China packages, lifting a ban imposed a day earlier
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — The U.S. Postal Service is reversing course a day after placing a ban on all inbound packages from China and Hong Kong. The post office had announced Tuesday that it would no longer accept parcels from the China and Hong Kong after the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: DeSantis goes to battle with Florida Republicans in trying to get closer to Trump
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — With an open presidential primary coming in 2028, Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is launching an offensive to win back supporters of the Republican leader whom he dared to challenge in the last election.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USAID direct hires put on leave worldwide, except those deemed essential
item.description: The Trump administration is placing U.S. Agency for International Development direct-hire staffers around the world on leave, except those deemed essential.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump floats US taking over Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip, saying America should be responsible for clearing the territory of rubble and unexploded bombs and proposing an “economic development.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Still locked out of federal funding, several Head Start preschools may need to close temporarily
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Head Start preschools serving thousands of children around the country could be at risk of closing because they remain locked out of their federal funding, a problem that first surfaced last week during President Donald Trump's aborted effort to freeze federal grants.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4
